scope.summary: E5 harness now proves fixed-TTL grant expiry and commit-in-doubt interruption on a real installed artifact lane using public, non-intrusive failure paths only.
out_of_scope: Product runtime changes to add dedicated commit-in-doubt or grant-expiry test hooks.
Any behavior not covered by this harness test's live-wire assertions.

entry_points.name: python3 scripts/rig/failure_recovery_e2e.py
entry_points.trace: Ran full wire-backed pass with ORACLEMCP_E5_GRANT_WAIT_SECONDS=312 and ORACLEMCP_E5_SKIP_GRANT_EXPIRY_CHECK=0 against installed artifact at source 9c9f857cc0e39e2493b11e004b8f5535f0468e3b.

entry_points.name: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m py_compile scripts/rig/failure_recovery_e2e.py
entry_points.trace: Harness script compiles cleanly after scoped edits.

entry_points.name: git show --stat HEAD
entry_points.trace: Verified landed commit scope is only scripts/rig/failure_recovery_e2e.py and tests/artifacts/evidence/e5-failure-recovery-e2e.json.

limitations: No runtime product hooks were added for commit-in-doubt kill or grant expiry. Inductions stay on public-facing admin and wire-confirmation paths.
Grant expiry is proven by waiting against fixed 300-second confirmation TTL; we did not add a configurable TTL knob in this pass.
follow_ups: If stricter timing certainty is required for the 300-second wait window, add a deterministic scheduler-assisted grant-expiry test path in a separate bead and keep it out-of-band from product runtime behavior.
